already_exists.description: What to do if the table already exists accepts: fail, replace, append

column_data_types.description: [CSV only] A dictionary with column names and their data types if you need to change the defaults. Example: {'user_id':'int'}. Check Python Pandas library for supported data types

column_dates.description: [CSV and Excel only] A list of column names that should be parsed as dates. Example: date,timestamp

columns_read.description: A List of the column names that should be read

dataframe_index.description: Write dataframe index as a column.

day_first.description: [CSV only] DD/MM format dates, international and European format

decimal_character.description: [CSV and Excel only] Character to recognize as decimal point. Default is '.'

delimiter.description: [CSV only] The character used to separate values in the CSV file (e.g., a comma, semicolon, or tab).

header_row.description: [CSV and Excel only] Row containing the headers to use as column names (0 is first line of data). Leave empty if there is no header row.

index_column.description: [CSV and Excel only] Column to use as the row labels of the dataframe. Leave empty if no index column

null_values.description: [CSV and Excel only] A list of strings that should be treated as null. Examples: '' for empty strings, 'None', 'N/A', Warning: Hive database supports only a single value

rows_to_read.description: [CSV and Excel only] Number of rows to read from the file. If None, reads all rows.

schema.description: The schema to upload the data file to.

sheet_name.description: [Excel only]] Strings used for sheet names (default is the first sheet).

skip_blank_lines.description: [CSV only] Skip blank lines in the CSV file.

skip_rows.description: [CSV and Excel only] Number of rows to skip at start of file.

table_name.description: The name of the table to be created/appended
